description: Синхронизация skills go-microservice (обёртка) и coding-rules (база) с реальной архитектурой backend_core: gRPC-first нарратив, плоский domain/fx.go, исправление путей, новые reference-файлы (grpc-delivery, outbox-pattern, api-gateway-pattern), очистка corporate-специфики из coding-rules.

userStories.title: Прочитать существующие reference-файлы skills и сверить с backend_core
userStories.description: As a skills developer, I want to audit the current state of all skill files and canonical backend_core services so I know exactly which formulations to change.
acceptanceCriteria: Read all files src/skills/go-microservice/SKILL.md and src/skills/go-microservice/references/*.md
Read all files src/skills/coding-rules/golang/*.md
Read ~/Work/friday_releases/cryptoprocessing/backend_core/CLAUDE.md, order_service/CLAUDE.md, auth_gateway/CLAUDE.md
Produce a list of exact current formulations to change in each file (saved as a comment in audit-notes.md or in the agent's notes)
No file edits in this story — read-only fact-checking phase
userStories.notes: Audit results in tasks/audit-notes.md. Read all four skill files, three CLAUDE.md sources, and verified real layout via order_service/internal/. Confirmed several paths in skills are wrong (entities location, http_clients, workers vs worker, per-aggregate fx.go). Grep for corporate leaks in coding-rules captured. No skill files modified.

userStories.title: Очистить coding-rules от corporate-специфики
userStories.description: As an opensource skills user, I want coding-rules to remain a generic base with no mentions of corporate libraries or shared packages.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -rE 'shared/|pgconnector|kafkaconnector|squirrel|shopspring/decimal|redsync|access_level_guard|fasthttp|timetools.FrontendTime|outbox' src/skills/coding-rules/ returns 0 matches
Generic material accidentally living in go-microservice (general clean architecture, generic DDD, naming without library references) is moved to coding-rules or deleted as duplicate
Allowed generic additions only: 'money via arbitrary-precision library, not float' (without shopspring/decimal mention), 'typed errors preferred over sentinel-only'
src/skills/coding-rules/SKILL.md basic structure NOT changed
Markdown relative links resolve (no broken links)
userStories.notes: AC grep returns 0 matches (verified: exit code 1). Edits in: SKILL.md (framework table & reference summary), common.md (decimal lib mention), clean-architecture.md (decimal imports + OutboxPublisher rename), kafka.md (Outbox Pattern → Transactional Event Publication; CDC section words updated), redis.md (section 9 entirely rewritten as generic Redlock guidance, no library mentioned), grpc.md (1-line wording), patterns.md (1-line see-also), postgres.md (1-line wording), http.md (removed fasthttp row + heuristic mention; rewrote fiber description). SKILL.md structure preserved. No broken markdown relative links (none exist in coding-rules). No generic material from go-microservice moved in this story — that work is scheduled for US-003..US-011 per the audit's story→file impact summary.

userStories.title: Переписать SKILL.md под gRPC-first нарратив
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want to see gRPC as primary transport and HTTP only in API gateway context, so I do not get wrong recommendations when creating new services.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skills/go-microservice/SKILL.md replaces HTTP-first narrative with gRPC-first
Removed mentions of 'one aggregate = one fx.go'
Canonical domain/fx.go pattern fixed: flat assembly + side-modules
Added cross-references to api-gateway-pattern.md, outbox-pattern.md, grpc-delivery.md (these files may not yet exist — references resolve once created in later stories)
Markdown without broken relative links (excluding refs to files yet-to-be-created)

userStories.title: Переписать template-structure.md под реальную раскладку backend_core
userStories.description: As a developer creating a new service, I want template-structure to match order_service 1-to-1 so following the skill produces the correct directory layout.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/entity/ fixed at internal/ level (not inside domain)
internal/infrastructure/ rewritten: grpc/{server,clients/{...}}/, kafka/{consumer,producer}/, worker/, outbox/, optional http/server/, optional postgres/
module <service-name> (kebab-case) and Go 1.25+ explicitly stated
Table 'Pattern X across Y services' with examples from 7 services (side-vs-core split)
domain/fx.go section shows flat fx.Provide for all use-cases of main domain
Side-modules section (audit/email/outbox/worker) with real-service examples
Migrations section describes both db/ and migrations/
Configs section indicates .local.env + k8s values
Dockerfile, Makefile, README.md, docker-compose.yml, docs/swagger.* marked optional
Each major section starts with 'Generic basis:' link to coding-rules + 'Corporate delta:' content
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Переписать layer-patterns.md под gRPC-first и реальные паттерны
userStories.description: As a developer, I want layer-patterns to describe gRPC-first delivery, repository/usecase/errors with corporate specifics, and explicit service start/stop sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Delivery section: gRPC primary + Kafka + HTTP (gateway-only subsection)
Repository section: pgconnector.IDB.Do(ctx) + db.WithTx + squirrel/goqu builders
UseCase section: logger.ILogger.InfowCtx, shopspring/decimal for money
Errors section: gRPC mapping (MapError → status.Error(codes.*)) primary, HTTP moved to gateway reference
Workers section: claim pattern (ClaimPendingReports style) + multi-instance (ResumeWithdrawOrderWorker × 3)
Separate 'Service start/stop sequence' section: observability → DB/Redis → external gRPC clients → domain → kafka consumer → workers → gRPC/HTTP servers → healthcheck
DTO section: Validate() without mutation, ApplyDefaults() separate
Each section starts with 'Generic basis:' link to coding-rules/golang/*.md
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Обновить internal-packages.md под реальные shared-зависимости
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the precise list of shared packages from backend_core and correct infrastructure client paths (gRPC, not HTTP).
acceptanceCriteria: Removed all mentions of repository/http_clients/
Added infrastructure/grpc/clients/{service}/ pattern with per-client fx.go and fx.Annotate(NewClient, fx.As(new(deps.XClient)))
shared packages table verified against actual go.mod imports of 5 services
Redis section: shared/redisconnector single source, redsync mentioned only in gateway-pattern
Kafka section: shared/kafkaconnector, AddSimpleJSONHandler, late handler registration
Observability section: loggerfx.LoggerFx, tracerfx.TracerFx, meterfx.MeterFx + OTel conventions
External integrations section: go.uber.org/ratelimit, go-resty/resty/v2 for exchange-services
Each section starts with 'Generic basis:' link to coding-rules
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Обновить new-domain-checklist.md под реальные пути и gRPC
userStories.description: As a developer adding a new domain, I want a checklist that leads to the right place — no typos, gRPC handlers, single domain/fx.go rule.
acceptanceCriteria: usecase/buissines/ fixed to usecase/business/ (+ flat usecase/ for simple domains)
Entities moved to internal/entity/ level
HTTP-handlers checklist replaced with gRPC-handlers checklist
Explicit rule: new use case in main domain → fx.Provide added to existing internal/domain/fx.go, no new file created
Side-module indicators listed (when separate {X}/fx.go is needed)
Optional outbox step added for domains with external events
Canonical worker example: worker/ (no 's')
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Создать references/grpc-delivery.md
userStories.description: As a gRPC-service developer, I want a detailed reference for gRPC delivery layer with real corporate patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/skills/go-microservice/references/grpc-delivery.md created
'Generic basis' section links to coding-rules/golang/grpc.md (or notes 'TODO: extract generic part to coding-rules' if file doesn't exist)
'Corporate delta' section: access_level_guard, dual external/internal services, corporate mapError → codes.*
Server lifecycle via fx.Invoke and fx.Hook{OnStart,OnStop} described
delivery/grpc/handlers.go structure with handler example
Server registration patterns documented
Cross-reference to order_service example
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Создать references/outbox-pattern.md
userStories.description: As a developer of services with external events (order_service, exchange_service, auth_gateway), I want to understand outbox CDC structure and shared/outbox integration.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/skills/go-microservice/references/outbox-pattern.md created
Structure internal/domain/{...}/outbox/{notify,netpublisher}/ described
Repository integration: write to outbox table inside db.WithTx
Outbox worker described (reads table, publishes to Kafka)
PRODUCER_OUTBOX_* config parameters listed
Distinction internal/infrastructure/outbox/ vs internal/domain/.../outbox/ explained
Cross-reference to order_service example
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Создать references/api-gateway-pattern.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to understand API gateway specifics (auth_gateway) — a separate architectural class from gRPC microservices.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/skills/go-microservice/references/api-gateway-pattern.md created
internal/infrastructure/grpc/proxy/ described (HTTP-to-gRPC reverse proxy to 11 backend services)
Stack described: fasthttp + fasthttp/router + 3 middleware groups
References to pkg/jwtutil, pkg/secure
Gateway-only libraries described: fernet/fernet-go, golang-jwt/jwt/v5, pquerna/otp, go-redsync/redsync/v4
Swagger pipeline described (swaggo/swag, swag init)
Note that HTTP utils (pkg/httputil.WriteResponse) live only here
Cross-reference to auth_gateway example
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Применить delta-структуру ко всем reference-файлам go-microservice
userStories.description: As a skill reader, I want a unified 'Generic basis → Corporate delta' structure in every reference file so I understand what is universal and what is backend_core-specific.
acceptanceCriteria: In layer-patterns.md, internal-packages.md, template-structure.md, new-domain-checklist.md, grpc-delivery.md, outbox-pattern.md, api-gateway-pattern.md every major section starts with 'Generic basis: see coding-rules/golang/{X}.md'
'Corporate delta' subsection contains only what differs from generic
Any duplicate generic material from coding-rules removed
Mapping table from PRD applied to all file pairs
Markdown relative links resolve

userStories.title: Mental dry-run: создание нового домена по обновлённому checklist'у
userStories.description: As a skill QA, I want to mentally walk through new-domain-checklist.md to confirm it leads to the correct location.
acceptanceCriteria: Mental dry-run executed: 'create new domain in order_service' following the new checklist
Each checklist step verified against real order_service/internal/domain/{order,masspay,...}/ structure
Discrepancies (if any) recorded and fixed in new-domain-checklist.md
Result documented as a short note in the agent's progress

userStories.title: Cross-check на mailer_service и auth_gateway
userStories.description: As a skill QA, I want to verify updated template-structure.md and layer-patterns.md match not only order_service but also a simple service (mailer_service) and gateway (auth_gateway).
acceptanceCriteria: template-structure.md compared with mailer_service/ (simple Kafka-consumer)
api-gateway-pattern.md compared with auth_gateway/
Discrepancies (if any) recorded and fixed in the relevant skill files
Result documented as a short note in the agent's progress

userStories.title: Проверить linter-нейтральность markdown skills
userStories.description: As a user, I want all skill markdown files to have no broken relative links.
acceptanceCriteria: All relative links between go-microservice reference files verified
Links from go-microservice → coding-rules verified
Broken links (if any) fixed
Used a script or grep to confirm: every `](...)` link target exists

userStories.title: Обновить MEMORY.md если содержит затронутые утверждения
userStories.description: As a skill process, I want to remove outdated memory records that contradict the new understanding of backend_core architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: MEMORY.md read (if exists)
Statements about per-aggregate fx.go, HTTP-first, deps/dep.go, buissines spelling, http_clients pattern updated or removed
If new memory records are needed, added via Write following the memory frontmatter format
If MEMORY.md does not exist or has no relevant entries, story passes with a note explaining no changes needed

userStories.title: Финальный git diff review
userStories.description: As a skills developer, before committing I want to verify by eye that no useful generic explanations were deleted, only specialized for backend_core.
acceptanceCriteria: git diff src/skills/ reviewed line-by-line
No deleted generic explanation that does not have a counterpart in coding-rules
All edits only specialize, not throw away
Commit created via the commit skill (Conventional Commits format with ticket ID)
